headline: Strategic Thinking is Lowkey the Most Important Consulting Skill Right Now—Here's What You Need to Know

stories.title: Master Strategic Thinking to Actually Move the Needle in Consulting
stories.summary: Strategic thinking—the ability to see big picture problems, anticipate challenges, and develop long-term solutions—is dominating 2026 as the must-have consulting skill. Organizations that invest in developing this competency see measurable improvements in execution, innovation, and resilience, especially with AI disruption happening everywhere.
stories.takeaway: Stop focusing on just executing tasks; learn to frame problems strategically and understand how different business functions connect. Practice MECE thinking and scenario planning to separate yourself from other consultants.

stories.title: Excel's New Formula Completion Feature Literally Writes Your Formulas for You
stories.summary: Powered by AI, Excel's new formula completion feature now suggests and autocompletes formulas as soon as you type '=', analyzing your sheet's context to propose accurate formulas. It's rolling out to Microsoft 365 users and lowkey saves hours on syntax errors and common tasks like lookups and counts.
stories.takeaway: Type '=' and let Copilot do the heavy lifting—especially powerful when your data has clear headers and labels. Press Tab or Enter to accept suggestions, but always review the formula before finalizing because AI suggestions aren't infallible.

stories.title: EY Just Dropped EY.ai PDLC—An AI-Native Software Delivery Platform That's Actually Game-Changing
stories.summary: EY unveiled EY.ai PDLC, a new AI-native approach to software delivery that compresses months-long development roadmaps into days. Powered by 8090's Software Factory, it automates manual coding tasks so consultants can focus on strategy—and EY's deploying it to tens of thousands of consultants right now.
stories.takeaway: The future of consulting isn't about writing code anymore; it's about leveraging AI tools that handle the heavy lifting so you can focus on high-level strategy and business impact. Start thinking about how your firm can integrate similar AI-native platforms into your workflow.

watchNext.title: OpenAI's Frontier Alliances: Four Consulting Giants Now Selling AI Enterprise Solutions
watchNext.why: OpenAI just partnered with BCG, McKinsey, Accenture, and Capgemini to sell OpenAI Frontier—a no-code platform for building and deploying AI agents. Fr worth watching because it shows how consulting is shifting from advisory-only to bundled tech + strategy delivery.

watchNext.title: AI Adoption Just Hit 78% of Organizations—And the Biggest Gap is Between Theory and Reality
watchNext.why: Anthropic's new research shows AI can theoretically handle 94% of tasks in computer/math roles, but only 33% are actually deployed in practice. That workflow friction, legal constraints, and org resistance? Those are the biggest consulting opportunities in 2026.
